---
title: "Private Estimation"
subtitle: "Auditing failed estimators to design mechanisms"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: white
    slide-number: true
    embed-resources: false
    code-overflow: wrap
    scrollable: true
    css: slides.css
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
  echo: false
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
#| include: false
try:
    ip = get_ipython()
    if "autoreload" not in ip.extension_manager.loaded:
        ip.run_line_magic("load_ext", "autoreload")
        ip.run_line_magic("autoreload", "2")
except Exception:
    pass

try:
    import libdpy
except ImportError:
    %pip install -q "libdpy[notebook] @ git+https://github.com/applied-dp-course/pub_lib.git"
    import libdpy

%matplotlib inline
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
#| include: false
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

from libdpy.assignment_specific.private_estimation.lecture_figures import (
    make_audit_empirical_mu_sigma_clipping_figure,
    make_audit_empirical_quantile_clipping_figure,
    make_audit_raw_mean_figure,
    make_compare_empirical_vs_private_quantile_clipping_figure,
    make_gaussian_method_comparison_figure,
    make_median_ordinary_vs_worst_case_figure,
    make_oracle_income_histogram_figure,
    make_rank_loss_median_figure,
    EPS_LOW_Q,
    EPS_HIGH_Q,
    EPS_MEAN,
)

SEED = 42
EPS_TOTAL = EPS_LOW_Q + EPS_HIGH_Q + EPS_MEAN
```

## From auditing to mechanisms

::: {.fragment}
We audit **our own proposals** to design mechanisms.
:::

::: {.fragment}
Running problem: estimate typical **income** from Fulton PUMS microdata.
:::

::: {.fragment}
Loop: propose → audit → diagnose → repair.
:::

## Public-menu contract

::: {.fragment}
Every grid, bin edge, threshold, and transform is **fixed before** private data is touched.
:::

::: {.fragment}
Public data can choose the **menu**; private data must **privately choose from the menu**.
:::

## Oracle EDA — not a private release

```{python}
fig = make_oracle_income_histogram_figure(seed=SEED)
fig
```

::: {.notes}
Exact histograms leak bin and threshold choices. The private repair arrives later.
:::

## Raw noisy mean fails

```{python}
fig = make_audit_raw_mean_figure(seed=SEED)
fig
```

::: {.fragment}
Diagnosis: unbounded contribution — the mean is not a query waiting for noise.
:::

## Empirical quantile clipping fails

```{python}
fig = make_audit_empirical_quantile_clipping_figure(seed=SEED)
fig
```

::: {.fragment}
Thresholds were learned non-privately; noise on the final number does not fix that.
:::

## Median: robust ≠ DP-stable

```{python}
fig = make_median_ordinary_vs_worst_case_figure(seed=SEED)
fig
```

## Repair 1: rank-error quantile

```{python}
fig = make_rank_loss_median_figure(seed=SEED)
fig
```

::: {.fragment}
Privacy from changing the **error metric**, not only adding noise.
:::

## Repair 2: private quantile-clipped mean

```{python}
fig = make_compare_empirical_vs_private_quantile_clipping_figure(seed=SEED)
fig
```

::: {.fragment}
Budget: $\varepsilon_{\mathrm{low}}+\varepsilon_{\mathrm{high}}+\varepsilon_{\mathrm{mean}} = `r EPS_TOTAL`$.
:::

## Empirical μ±kσ fails

```{python}
fig = make_audit_empirical_mu_sigma_clipping_figure(seed=SEED)
fig
```

## Repair 3: noisy histogram localization

```{python}
fig = make_gaussian_method_comparison_figure(seed=SEED)
fig
```

::: {.fragment}
Privacy from mechanisms; **accuracy** from the Gaussian assumption.
:::

## Takeaways

- Every data-dependent choice is part of the release.
- Auditing disproves claims; it never proves DP.
- Witness construction is the practical skill.
- Next: choosing **which** query to answer (Lecture 6).
